2603.17775.title: CoVerRL: Breaking the Consensus Trap in Label-Free Reasoning via Generator-Verifier Co-Evolution
2603.17775.paper_summary: Label-free reinforcement learning enables large language models to improve reasoning capabilities without ground-truth supervision, typically by treating majority-voted answers as pseudo-labels. However, we identify a critical failure mode: as training maximizes self-consistency, output diversity collapses, causing the model to confidently reinforce systematic errors that evade detection. We term this the consensus trap. To escape it, we propose CoVerRL, a framework where a single model alternates between generator and verifier roles, with each capability bootstrapping the other. Majority voting provides noisy but informative supervision for training the verifier, while the improving verifier progressively filters self-consistent errors from pseudo-labels. This co-evolution creates a virtuous cycle that maintains high reward accuracy throughout training. Experiments across Qwen and Llama model families demonstrate that CoVerRL outperforms label-free baselines by 4.7-5.9\% on mathematical reasoning benchmarks. Moreover, self-verification accuracy improves from around 55\% to over 85\%, confirming that both capabilities genuinely co-evolve.
2603.17775.paper_summary_zh: 無標籤強化學習使大型語言模型能夠在沒有真實監督的情況下提高推理能力，通常通過將多數投票的答案視為偽標籤。然而，我們識別出一個關鍵的失效模式：隨著訓練最大化自我一致性，輸出多樣性崩潰，導致模型自信地強化逃避檢測的系統性錯誤。我們將其稱為共識陷阱。為了逃脫這一陷阱，我們提出了CoVerRL，一個框架，其中單個模型在生成器和驗證器角色之間交替，每種能力相互啟動。多數投票為訓練驗證器提供了嘈雜但有用的監督，而不斷改進的驗證器逐步過濾偽標籤中的自我一致性錯誤。這種共同演化創造了一個良性循環，保持了訓練過程中高獎勵準確性。在Qwen和Llama模型系列的實驗中，證明CoVerRL在數學推理基準上比無標籤基準提高了4.7-5.9\%。此外，自我驗證準確率從約55\%提高到超過85\%，確認了這兩種能力確實是共同演化的。
2603.17775.author: Teng Pan et.al.
2603.17775.authors: Teng Pan, Yuchen Yan, Zixuan Wang, Ruiqing Zhang, Gaiyang Han, Wanqi Zhang, Weiming Lu, Jun Xiao, Yongliang Shen
